_comment: Auto-generated by scripts/build-draft-articles-json.py. Do not hand-edit — your changes will be overwritten on next deploy. To add a draft: drop articles/draft-{slug}.html (or set draft:true in articles.json) and run `make drafts`.

drafts.title: What Littoralicious Stands For — The Three Pillars, Written Down
drafts.lede: Three pillars, eight principles, one mission. The DNA of Littoralicious — the grandmother, the scientist, the yacht chef — written down so every article answers to the same standard. With the banned words, the voice rules, the design lock, the 11-PM test that every piece must pass before publication.

drafts.title: Focaccia: The Science-First Version
drafts.lede: Eighty-percent hydration, a 24-to-72-hour cold ferment, and a salamoia brine poured into the dimples. Three science-backed modifications that produce an open, custardy crumb and a crisp-tender crust on a single sheet pan. Every step tested, every claim sourced.

drafts.title: The 730-Plate Problem — A Working Method for Cooking for Crew
drafts.lede: A yacht chef serves 730 plates per crew member per year — same nine faces, no menu to hide behind. The mistake is to call it casual. The opportunity is touchstone-driven cooking: named real-world anchors, a weekly cadence built on Blue-Zone weekdays and ugly-delicious Fridays, and the nationality plates that make a crew member text their mother.
